$schema_note: Misconception suggestions harvested by MisconceptionAnalyst from common_miss in data/buckets/adjective_agreement.json. FOLDED per inter_chat/Architecture_MisconceptionAnalyst_registry_harvest.md v2: the two cross-kind orthography pairs are tagged into the EXISTING verb specifics (no sibling ids) per ruling A. Governance: analyst proposes, architecture ratifies.

proposed_family_actions.note: adjective position is word_order's first specific.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Adjective fails to agree with its noun
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Leaving an adjective in the wrong gender or number for the noun it modifies, where the adjective is of the right inflection class.
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: Flagship agreement family; co-occurs with participle and clitic agreement.
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Pure agreement-value error; kept distinct from regularisation.adjective_class.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Mixed-gender plural not defaulted to masculine
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: With a mixed-gender plural group, agreeing with the nearest noun instead of using the masculine-plural default.
examples: i ragazzi e le ragazze sono italiane (for italiani)
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: CROSS-KIND: identical pattern in passato participle agreement (sono andate for andati). ONE shared specific, tagged from both trees.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Wrong allomorph of a pre-nominal form (bello/buono/quello)
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Selecting the wrong allomorph of an adjective that inflects like an article before the noun, choosing by the following sound.
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: CROSS-KIND-IN-WAITING: same sound-conditioned selection as the articles (il/lo/l').
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Bridge specific into the article tree later.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Class II (-e) adjective inflected as Class I (-o/-a)
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Treating an -e-class adjective (grande) as if it took the dominant -o/-a Class I pattern.
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: Same regularisation shape as regularisation.irregular_verb / irregular_participle.
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Wrong class altogether, not a gender/number mis-value.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Inflecting an adjective that should stay invariable: noun-derived colours (rosa, viola) and compound colour expressions (verde scuro).
examples: le pareti verdi scure / verde scure (for verde scuro)
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: Over-application of the agreement rule (kept per ruling D).
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Mirror of regularisation.irregular_*: an invariable wrongly made to agree.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Misapplying the -co plural split (-chi hard vs -ci soft, by stress and lexical irregularity): over-applying one where the other is required.
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: No existing verb equivalent (this is the which-way-does-the-rule-go error), so kept as a new specific (not affected by ruling A).
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Distinct from the unified omitted_h specific: here the rule's direction is the question.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Adjective placed before the noun where post-nominal is default
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Fronting a descriptive adjective on the English pattern, where Italian's unmarked order is post-nominal.
examples: la rossa casa (for la casa rossa)
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: First word_order specific. Co-tags with transfer (English order) now that transfer is an overlay tag (v2 B).

reuses_existing.defined_in: data/misconceptions.json (v1 seed; description to be broadened to kind-neutral per v2 A)
reuses_existing.note: bianci/lungi = the SAME hard-c/g h-rule as the verb cerchi. Unified per ruling A; tagged into the existing specific, no sibling created.

reuses_existing.defined_in: data/misconceptions.json (v1 seed; to be broadened per v2 A)
reuses_existing.note: vecchii/grigii/liscie = the SAME redundant-i rule as the verb mangii. Unified per ruling A.
